label: Multimodal foundation models

node.label: Multimodal foundation models
node.definition: A multimodal foundation model aligns and jointly processes text, images, audio, video, or actions through shared token spaces and cross-modal interaction.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Multimodal foundation models is modeled as depending on Transformers, Cross-attention & co-attention and feeding downstream nodes such as Multimodal assistants & vision-language systems, Browser, desktop & computer-use agents, Robotics & embodied AI.
examples: atlas downstream link to Multimodal assistants & vision-language systems
atlas downstream link to Browser, desktop & computer-use agents
atlas downstream link to Robotics & embodied AI
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Transformers, Cross-attention & co-attention.

dependencies.rationale: Multimodal foundation models extends or specializes the architecture family Transformers.
In this atlas graph, Multimodal foundation models has a `composes` dependency on Cross-attention & co-attention with `core` strength.

In this atlas graph, Multimodal foundation models has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Contrastive and mutual-information objectives because Contrastive and mutual-information objectives is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.
Multimodal foundation models consumes, emits, or organizes information using Multimodal, simulated & streaming data.
In this atlas graph, Multimodal foundation models has a `composes` dependency on VQ-VAE & learned discrete tokenizers with `common` strength.
